vocab.meaning: tạo ra sự công bằng (trong thể thao đồng đội, sự phối hợp giúp bù đắp kỹ năng cá nhân)
vocab.example: In team sports, superior strategy can often level the playing field even if the opposing side has more talented individuals.

vocab.meaning: tự lực dựa vào bản lĩnh của chính mình
vocab.example: Solo athletes are constantly on their own mettle, as there is no teammate to provide a safety net during a dip in performance.

vocab.meaning: tình thế có thể thay đổi bất ngờ (thường dùng cho các môn đồng đội)
vocab.example: Football is truly a game of two halves; a team can look defeated initially but win through collective spirit in the end.

vocab.meaning: thức khuya làm việc/tập luyện cực khổ
vocab.example: Individual sports stars often burn the midnight oil in solitary training sessions to perfect a single movement.

vocab.word: get into the swing of things
vocab.meaning: bắt nhịp với guồng quay/sự phối hợp
vocab.example: It takes time for new players to get into the swing of things and understand the unspoken cues of their teammates.

vocab.meaning: giữ vững sự lạc quan khi gặp khó khăn
vocab.example: In a team, peers help you keep your chin up after a mistake, whereas a solo golfer must process failure in isolation.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực vượt mức mong đợi
vocab.example: Individual athletes must go the extra mile in self-discipline because they lack a coach or captain watching their every move.

vocab.meaning: có quyền quyết định, làm chủ tình thế
vocab.example: A tennis player gets to call all the shots regarding their strategy, without needing to consult or compromise with a group.

vocab.meaning: có cùng suy nghĩ/thấu hiểu nhau
vocab.example: Success in doubles matches relies heavily on both partners being on the same wavelength at all times.

vocab.meaning: sự may rủi (trong việc bốc thăm đối thủ/đồng đội)
vocab.example: In individual tournaments, your progress often depends on the luck of the draw regarding who you face in the opening rounds.
